concepts.title: Gravitational Field as a Field of Force
concepts.body: Show an understanding of a gravitational field as a field of force.

children.title: Definition of Gravitational Field Strength
children.body: Define gravitational field strength as force per unit mass.

concepts.title: Newton's Law of Gravitation
concepts.body: State and use Newton's law of gravitation in the form F = Gm1m2/r².

concepts.title: Gravitational Field of a Point Mass
concepts.body: Understand the characteristics and equations for the gravitational field generated by a point mass.

children.title: Derivation of g = Gm/r²
children.body: Derive from Newton's law of gravitation and the definition of gravitational field strength, the equation g = Gm/r² for the gravitational field strength of a point mass.

children.title: Using g = Gm/r²
children.body: Use the equation g = Gm/r² for the gravitational field strength of a point mass.

concepts.title: Gravitational Field Near Earth's Surface
concepts.body: Explain that on the surface of the Earth g is approximately constant and is called the acceleration of free fall.

children.title: Experiment to Determine Acceleration of Free Fall
children.body: Describe an experiment to determine the acceleration of free fall using a falling body.

concepts.title: Gravitational Potential
concepts.body: Understand the concept of gravitational potential.

children.title: Definition of Gravitational Potential
children.body: Define potential at a point as the work done in bringing unit mass from infinity to the point.

children.title: Using Gravitational Potential Equation
children.body: Use the equation φ = -Gm/r for the potential in the field of a point mass.

concepts.title: Analysis of Circular Orbits in Inverse Square Law Fields
concepts.body: Analyse circular orbits in inverse square law fields by relating the gravitational force to the centripetal acceleration it causes.

concepts.title: Everyday Applications of Gravitational Force
concepts.body: Describe and explain everyday applications of the gravitational force of attraction (include satellite and period of rotation).
